compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## What
Adopt the B1 batch primitive (`_store.batch_stage_and_commit`, ticket 890d-4733-1c88-4976 —
**hard prerequisite; this ticket `depends_on` B1**) on the NDJSON importer's independent
passes so a bulk import commits far fewer commits than one-per-event.

## Why
`src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py` already has idempotency-by-`source_id` and deferred-push,
but each pass funnels through a per-event commit. Every write in the importer — CREATE
(`composer.create_core`), parent/edit (`composer.edit_core`), comments and file-impact/
verify (`_commands/leaf.py`), links (`graph._links.add_dependency`) — ultimately calls the
ONE sink `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py::append_event`, which composes the event and commits
it via `event_append.write_and_push`. That single sink is where the commit happens.

## Deferred-commit sink (mechanism — a contextvar at ONE boundary, NOT kwarg-threading)
Introduce the compose/commit split at the SINGLE `_seam.append_event` boundary via a
module-level `contextvars.ContextVar` `_batch_sink` (default `None`):
- **`_batch_sink is None` (default):** `append_event` composes AND commits per-event exactly
  as today. Every existing caller — interactive and library — is unchanged, with NO kwarg
  threaded through `create_core`/`edit_core`/`leaf.*`/`_lib_writes`/`_lib_gates`. This is why
  the edit surface is tiny and guardrail-1 (interactive writes stay one-commit-per-event) is
  structurally guaranteed: nothing sets the contextvar outside the importer.
- **`_batch_sink` set to a buffer (a `list[tuple[str, dict]]`):** `append_event` still lets
  the caller's state-reading guards run (they execute BEFORE it) and still composes the
  canonical event, but APPENDS the composed `(ticket_id, event_dict)` to the buffer instead
  of committing. The importer owns the buffer, sets the contextvar around one pass, then
  flushes via `batch_stage_and_commit` and resets the contextvar.

## Flush-boundary failure semantics
The existing importer wraps each per-row write in a `try/except` that `warn()`s and
continues (fail-open at row granularity). That is PRESERVED for row-level problems by
moving them earlier: a malformed row that fails to COMPOSE (bad field, validation) is
`warn()`-skipped at compose time and simply never enters the buffer — same observable
behavior as today. The batch FLUSH itself (`batch_stage_and_commit`) is all-or-nothing:
a git-level flush failure aborts the import with a clear error rather than silently
dropping a chunk. This is safe because the import already runs `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off`,
is idempotent-by-`source_id`, and is crash-resumable — a failed/partial flush leaves
whole-commit-or-none and a re-run re-scans committed `source_id`s and resumes.

## Scope (edit surface)
- `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py` — add the `_batch_sink` contextvar + a small helper
  (`batch_sink(buffer)` contextmanager) and the "append to sink vs commit" branch in
  `append_event`. This is the ONLY write-path source file changed.
- `src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py` — for the independent, append-only passes **Pass 1
  (CREATE), Pass 2a (parents), Pass 2c (file-impact/verify-commands), Pass 2d (comments)**,
  wrap each pass in `batch_sink(buf)` and flush `buf` through `batch_stage_and_commit` in
  chunks (default 256, hard-coded — no new config key). Passes run sequentially and each
  pass's buffer is flushed (committed) before the next, so a later pass reads the prior
  pass's committed state.
- **Pass 2b (links) and Pass 2e (statuses) stay per-event (do NOT set the contextvar):**
  2b because `add_dependency` does read-after-write within the pass (it emits TWO
  `_write_link_event` calls for a reciprocal `relates_to` and calls `_is_active_link`, an
  idempotency check that READS committed link state — buffering would make a later link in
  the chunk unable to observe an earlier one, a TOCTOU); 2e because it is STATEFUL
  (children-before-parents, `cascade=False`).

## Commit-count math
Per-pass flush -> total NEW commits = `sum_over_batched_passes(ceil(pass_events/256))` +
(per-event commits from Pass 2b links and Pass 2e statuses). For a synthetic import of N=3000 tickets where each ticket has 1 CREATE + 1 comment and
**NO parents, file-impact, verify-commands, links, or status transitions** (so Pass 1 and
Pass 2d each carry 3000 events and Passes 2a/2b/2c/2e carry zero), that is
`ceil(3000/256) + ceil(3000/256) = 12 + 12 = 24` batch commits vs ~6000 per-event (>=10x
fewer). The benchmark asserts the DELTA (commits after − before; baseline `init_repo`
commits excluded). A synthetic file that also exercises parents/file-impact adds
`ceil(pass_2a/256) + ceil(pass_2c/256)` more batch commits.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_batch_sink` contextvar + `batch_sink()` contextmanager in `src/rebar/_commands/_seam.py`; `append_event` appends the composed `(ticket_id, event_dict)` to the active sink and skips the commit when a sink is set, else commits per-event exactly as today (default path byte-for-byte unchanged)
- [ ] Guardrail 1 preserved: only the importer ever sets the contextvar; interactive writes (`create`/`edit`/`transition`/`claim`/`link`/`comment`) still commit one-event-per-commit (a unit test asserts a normal `create`/`comment` still makes one commit each)
- [ ] Importer Passes 1, 2a, 2c, 2d run under `batch_sink(buf)` and flush via `batch_stage_and_commit` in 256-event chunks; Pass 2b (links) and Pass 2e (statuses) remain per-event
- [ ] Equivalence PROVEN by a pytest that imports the same NDJSON into two trackers (per-event vs batched) and asserts equal replayed state via a concrete normalizer: for each ticket id, load `rebar.show_ticket(tid, repo_root=t)` (the reducer's replayed dict) from both stores, delete the volatile keys (`created_at`, `updated_at`, `env_id`, and every event/comment `uuid`+`timestamp`), and assert the two normalized dicts are equal for every ticket (a raw `git ls-tree` diff is NOT valid because event filenames embed per-run `uuid`/`timestamp`). Concretely: a test helper `_state(t) = {tid: _normalize(rebar.show_ticket(tid, repo_root=t)) for tid in rebar.list_tickets(repo_root=t)}` with `_normalize` deleting the volatile keys, and `assert _state(t_per_event) == _state(t_batched)`
- [ ] Benchmark: a synthetic N=3000 import where each ticket has only 1 CREATE + 1 comment (no parents/file-impact/verify/links/statuses, so only Pass 1 and Pass 2d carry events) makes the commit-count delta = `2*ceil(3000/256)` (=24), >=10x fewer than the ~6000 per-event commits. Commit count is read via `int(subprocess.run(["git","-C",tracker,"rev-list","--count","HEAD"],capture_output=True,text=True).stdout)` before and after the import
- [ ] Idempotent re-import test: run the same NDJSON import twice into one tracker; assert the second run adds ZERO commits (`rev-list --count HEAD` unchanged) and `rebar.list_tickets` returns the same ids/count (source_id re-scan skips all already-imported events)
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && python -m pytest tests/ -k import -q` all green

file_impact.reason: add _batch_sink contextvar + batch_sink() contextmanager; append_event branches to sink vs commit
wrap independent passes (1,2a,2c,2d) in batch_sink and flush via batch_stage_and_commit in chunks

compiled_state.title: B2: route the NDJSON importer's independent passes through the batch primitive
